source.multipart_basis: The original sample already specifies body_count=assembly with 'concrete and each FRP bar as distinct bodies'. Multipart decomposition formalizes that split into concrete section, GFRP longitudinal bars, GFRP/steel stirrups, and load-point bearing pads.

full_prompt:
Design the ACI CN6 GFRP-reinforced concrete beam as a multi-part assembly, explicitly modeling the 1220 mm long 150 x 200 mm beam as the physical collection of the concrete section, GFRP longitudinal reinforcement bars, transverse stirrups, and load-point bearing pads. The original prompt already calls out 'deliver STEP AP242 assembly with concrete and each FRP bar as distinct bodies'; this multipart sidecar formalizes that decomposition.

The assembly consists of four part families: (A) the concrete beam body, a rectangular 1220 mm long x 150 mm wide x 200 mm deep section of normal-weight concrete with fc' 27.6 MPa; (B) GFRP longitudinal reinforcement bars placed near the tension face (bottom) and optionally compression face (top) of the beam on the team-selected bar schedule (typical 2-4 #4 or #5 GFRP bars per layer); (C) GFRP or steel stirrups spaced along the beam to provide shear capacity within the four-point bending geometry (typical #3 stirrups at 50-100 mm spacing over the shear span); (D) two load-point bearing pads on the top surface at 305 mm separation that distribute the 8.9 kN total four-point load into the beam without local crushing.

Geometry inherits the original sample verbatim: total length 1220 mm (48 in), clear span 1016 mm (40 in), cross-section 150 mm wide x 200 mm deep, four-point load separation 305 mm.

Material inherits verbatim: concrete fc' 27.6 MPa, GFRP bars with ffu 690 MPa and Ef 44.8 GPa. Stirrups may be GFRP or steel per team selection; bearing pads are typically 50 mm x 150 mm steel plates.

Loads inherit verbatim: LC1 four-point bending with 8.9 kN total applied load distributed as two 4.45 kN point loads at 305 mm separation on the top surface.

Pass/fail criteria inherit verbatim from the original and apply at the assembly level: (R1) nominal flexural capacity per ACI CODE-440.11-22 Chapter 22 >= 8.9 kN total load (0.68 kN-m at critical section); (R2) failure mode must be compression-controlled (rho_f > rho_fb per ACI 440.11-22 Section 22.2.2); (R3) midspan deflection under 8.9 kN <= 5.6 mm (span/180); (R4) phi factor >= 0.55 per ACI 440.11-22 Table 21.2.2; (R5) total material cost <= $25 per beam. Added assembly-level targets under fem_check_policy.assembled_only: FRP-concrete bond DCR and load-pad bearing DCR must be reported; per-part stresses are reported for diagnostic purposes but do not gate pass/fail.

Vibration, thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are not required. An ACI 440.11-22 Chapter 22 moment-capacity spreadsheet is required outside the FEA for the closed-form sectional check. Deliver STEP AP242 of the assembly with each body (concrete section, each GFRP bar, each stirrup, each load-point bearing pad) as a distinct solid tagged with part_family (A..D) and instance_index, plus a structural report listing nominal moment capacity, total failure load, failure mode, midspan deflection at 8.9 kN, phi factor, total cost, and FRP-concrete bond + bearing-pad DCR.
short_prompt:
Design an ACI CN6 GFRP-reinforced concrete beam as a multi-part assembly (concrete section, GFRP longitudinal bars, stirrups, two steel load-point bearing pads) per ACI CODE-440.11-22 Chapter 22.

Total length 1220 mm (clear span 1016 mm), cross-section 150 mm wide x 200 mm deep, four-point load separation 305 mm. Concrete fc' 27.6 MPa; GFRP bars ffu 690 MPa, Ef 44.8 GPa.

Load case: LC1 four-point bending 8900 N total (two 4450 N point loads at 305 mm apart).

Pass/fail: nominal capacity >= 8900 N (0.68 kN-m at critical section); rho_f > rho_fb (compression-controlled); midspan deflection at design load <= 5.6 mm (span/180); phi factor >= 0.55 per Table 21.2.2; total cost <= $25; FEM at assembled level; bond + bearing-pad DCR <= 1.0.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: ACI CODE-440.11-22 Chapter 22 flexural capacity uses a closed-form equivalent rectangular stress block with rho_f/rho_fb branch logic, not FEA.

prompt.title: GFRP-reinforced concrete beam per ACI 440.11-22 (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: Multi-part 150x200x1220 mm GFRP-RC beam: concrete section (A) + longitudinal GFRP bars (B) + stirrups (C) + 2 load-point bearing pads (D). 1016 mm clear span, 8.9 kN four-point bending target, ACI 440.11-22 Chapter 22 compression-controlled failure, span/180 deflection, $25 cost cap.

parts.name: concrete_beam_body
parts.role: 1220 mm long x 150 mm wide x 200 mm deep rectangular concrete beam section of normal-weight fc' 27.6 MPa concrete; provides the compressive stress block and encases the GFRP reinforcement

parts.name: gfrp_longitudinal_bar
parts.role: GFRP longitudinal bar placed on the tension face (and optionally compression face) of the beam; provides the tension reinforcement to resist the 0.68 kN-m design moment
parts.count_note: 2-4 bars per layer per team selection; rho_f must exceed rho_fb for compression-controlled failure per R2
parts.material_hint: GFRP bar per ACI 440.11-22 material schedule

parts.name: stirrup
parts.role: transverse shear reinforcement stirrup spaced along the beam at team-selected pitch (typical 50-100 mm within the shear span); carries beam shear outside the pure-moment region
parts.material_hint: GFRP or ASTM A615 Grade 60 steel

parts.name: load_point_bearing_pad
parts.role: steel bearing pad at each of the two load points on the top surface (305 mm apart); distributes the 4.45 kN per load point into the concrete beam without local crushing

assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: beam coordinates: +x along length, +y along width, +z up (compression face)

load_cases.name: four_point_bending
load_cases.application: concentrated loads on top surface via bearing pads

boundary_conditions.supports: simply supported at 1016 mm clear span

objective.primary: pass all ACI 440.11-22 criteria under assembled_only FEM check

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: ACI 440.11-22 Chapter 22 equivalent to 0.68 kN-m at critical section
compression-controlled requires rho_f > rho_fb per ACI 440.11-22 Section 22.2.2
FRP-concrete bond (ACI 440.11-22 development length) and load-pad bearing capacity (ACI 318-19 Section 22.8)
Generated coverage gate: ensures prompt-level envelope, body-count, report-field, and part geometry constraints are represented by an explicit geometry audit experiment.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: solid concrete with embedded reinforcement or plane-stress with truss
analysis_types: nonlinear static with concrete damage plasticity (optional)

interfaces.load_path: GFRP longitudinal bar to concrete bond (rib + surface adhesion); transfers tensile axial force into the concrete compression block
stirrup tied to longitudinal bar; confines concrete and carries beam shear across the shear span
load-pad bearing contact on the concrete top surface; distributes the 4.45 kN load point into the beam

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled model. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: The original sample already calls out body_count=assembly with concrete and each FRP bar as distinct bodies; the multipart sidecar formalizes that into concrete section, GFRP longitudinal bars, stirrups, and load-point bearing pads.
notes.speculation_caveats: Bar count and stirrup count are team-selected within ACI 440.11-22 requirements for compression-controlled failure.

generated_eval_coverage.purpose: Add runnable CCX coverage kit for JSON-only multipart prompt.
generated_eval_coverage.note: Generated checks assert solver coverage for every FEA-grade requirement, every declared load case, and prompt-level geometry constraints; they are not a domain-specific certification model.
